question: Spread: Montenegro (-2.5)
description:
In the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 31 at 12:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Montenegro" if Montenegro win the game by 3 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Slovenia".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on fifa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Montenegro vs. Slovenia - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 31 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Slovenia's superior recent form and deeper talent pool drive trader consensus favoring them in this UEFA Nations League League C Group 4 opener against Montenegro on October 10. The visitors advanced to the Euro 2024 round of 16 with wins over Portugal and Denmark, boasting stars like RB Leipzig striker Benjamin Sesko and Atletico Madrid keeper Jan Oblak, while remaining unbeaten in World Cup qualifiers. Montenegro, hosting at Podgorica, struggles with a three-match losing streak and defensive frailties, though home advantage could spark an upset. No major injuries reported, but Slovenia's rest edge post-internationals tilts implied probabilities, reflecting historical head-to-head dominance.

description:

events.title: San Marino vs. Andorra - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for March 31 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus in the San Marino vs. Andorra UEFA Nations League D Group 1 matchup tilts heavily toward Andorra as favorites, driven by their superior FIFA ranking (155th vs. 211th), recent head-to-head dominance—including 1-0 and 3-0 wins in 2022 friendlies—and marginally better form with a draw against Liechtenstein after losses to stronger foes. San Marino, still seeking their first competitive win ever, shipped 10 goals across defeats to Latvia and Cyprus in September qualifiers, exposing defensive frailties. No significant injuries mar official team reports ahead of the October 14 clash at Stadio Olimpico, though San Marino's home edge rarely sways outcomes against elevated opposition. Upcoming fixtures intensify promotion stakes for the visitors.

question: Will Pacto Histórico win <18 seats in the Colombian Senate in the 2026 Colombian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats won by Pacto Historico in the Colombian Senate as a result of this election.

If the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by Pacto Historico in the Senate.

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

events.title: # of seats won by PH in Colombia Senate Election?
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Colombia on 8 March 2026.

This market will resolve according to the number of seats won by Pacto Historico in the Colombian Senate as a result of this election.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls project Pacto Histórico (PH) to secure 24-26 seats in Colombia's 2026 Senate election, fueling trader consensus at 98.4% implied probability for this range amid minimal viable alternatives. PH's dominance builds on its 2022 haul of 20 seats, steady voter coalitions drawn to progressive reforms, and opposition disarray, with parties like Centro Democrático and Conservatives struggling for unified lists. Recent surveys from multiple firms reinforce this trajectory, highlighting PH's edge in open-list voting dynamics. Challenges could emerge from a surprise center-right merger boosting turnout, Petro administration policy reversals, or regional scandals eroding urban support, though evidence remains slim for deviations beyond 27-29 seats (3.9%).

question: NCAA Tournament: Player to record triple-double?
description:
This market will resolve “Yes” if any player records a triple-double in any game during the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament (including First Four games). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purpose of this market, a triple-double is achieved when a player records double digits (10 or more) in three of the following statistical categories: points, rebounds, assists, steals, or blocks.
		
If the 2026 Men’s Basketball NCAA Tournament competition is cancelled, postponed after April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or it cannot be determined if a triple-double occurred within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “50-50”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the NCAA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Triple-doubles remain exceedingly rare in the NCAA Tournament, with zero recorded in the 2024 men's bracket and only a handful over the past decade amid slowed pace and elite defenses that stifle assists and rebounds. Traders' 85% implied probability on "No" reflects this history, amplified by the current field lacking versatile stat-stuffers like past UConn's Tristen Newton, who notched one pre-tournament. Star bigs such as Purdue's Zach Edey dominate boards but rarely distribute, while guards face perimeter clamps in Sweet 16 and Elite Eight matchups, prioritizing efficient scoring over volume playmaking. No injuries or recent form shifts signal a breakout threat, cementing crowd wisdom on the unlikelihood.

events.title: FC Bayern München vs. Manchester United WFC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for Wednesday, April 1, 2026 between FC Bayern München and Manchester United WFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bayern München's dominant home form in the UEFA Women's Champions League, coupled with their unbeaten streak across all competitions this season, anchors trader consensus at 65% for a home win against Manchester United WFC. The German champions lead the Frauen-Bundesliga by nine points, boasting a stout defense conceding just 0.8 goals per game, while United's away struggles—winless in their last three European road trips—cap their upset odds at 47.5%. Recent developments include Bayern regaining midfielder Linda Dallmann from minor injury for full training and United confirming forward Alessia Russo's fitness despite a knock; no major suspensions alter lineups, but Bayern's rest advantage post-light domestic schedule bolsters their edge over a fatigued United side. Draw pricing at 22% reflects historical low-scoring encounters between top continental clubs.

question: Map 1: Odd/Even Total Kills?
description:
This market will resolve to "Odd" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an odd number.

This market will resolve to "Even" if the total combined kills by both teams in Map 1 is an even number.

"Total combined kills" includes all kills recorded during the match across all rounds, including kills during regulation rounds and overtime rounds if applicable. Team kills (friendly fire) and self-inflicted deaths (e.g., falling damage, own grenade) do not count as kills for the purposes of this market.

If no kills are recorded in Map 1, or if Map 1 is canceled (not played at all), or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is never played due to forfeit, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is not played because the series result has already been determined (e.g., a team clinches the series before Map 1 is needed), this market will resolve to 50-50.

If Map 1 is remade, resolution will be based on the remade game only.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Counter-Strike: 33 vs Acend (BO3) - A1 Gaming League Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Semifinal 1 match between 33 and Acend in the A1 Gaming League Playoffs, initially scheduled for March 7 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "33" if 33 win the match against Acend.

This market will resolve to "Acend" if Acend win the match against 33.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

eventMetadata.context_description: Acend holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their BO3 playoff clash against 33 in the A1 Gaming League, driven by a 4-1 record over their last five maps compared to 33's inconsistent 2-3 stretch, including a recent upset loss. Head-to-head history favors Acend with two straight wins on similar maps like Mirage and Inferno, where their AWPer bodyy shines at 1.25 rating. No roster changes reported, but 33's IGL faces pressure after sloppy executes in qualifiers. Playoff intensity and server-side picks could spark an underdog run, yet Acend's rest advantage post-group stage bolsters implied probabilities around 70%. Momentum tilts toward the French squad amid tightening odds.

question: Will Fidesz–KDNP win the most national list votes in the 2026 Hungarian parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026. Hungary uses a mixed electoral system in which most voters cast two votes: one for a candidate in a single-member constituency and one for a national party list. 

This market will resolve according to the listed political party or coalition whose national candidate list receives the most valid national party list votes in this election.

This market will resolve solely based on national party list votes cast in this election. Votes cast for single-member constituencies will not be considered.

If two or more parties/coalitions tie for the most valid national party list votes in this election, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If any unlisted party wins the most national list votes in this election, or the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the official election results reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu). However, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may also suffice.

Note: If the Fidesz-KDNP does not contest this election with a joint national party list, the Fidesz-KDNP option will represent the national party list officially registered by Fidesz.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls from firms like Medián and Publicus show Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, leading Fidesz-KDNP by 8-10 points in Hungary's prospective 2026 parliamentary popular vote, fueling trader consensus at 71% for Tisza as winner. This reflects Magyar's surge via massive rallies—drawing tens of thousands—and Fidesz fatigue from economic stagnation, inflation, and EU tensions over rule-of-law disputes. Recent catalysts include a child protection scandal eroding Fidesz support and partial EU fund releases pressuring Prime Minister Orbán, though Fidesz retains organizational advantages. Markets price in Tisza momentum but note volatility ahead of the April 2026 vote.

question: Will Tisza win the national list vote in the 2026 Hungarian Parliamentary election by 9%+?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026. Hungary uses a mixed electoral system in which most voters cast two votes: one for a candidate in a single-member constituency and one for a national party list. 

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two parties/coalitions in terms of national party list votes in this election.

For the purposes of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid national list votes received by the party/coalition whose national candidate list wins the most national list votes and the party/coalition whose national candidate list wins the second-most national list votes. Percentages of the valid votes received by each party/coalition will be determined by dividing the total number of valid national list votes each of the top two parties/coalitions receives by the sum of all valid national party list votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve solely based on national party list votes cast in this election. Votes cast for single-member constituencies will not be considered.

If two or more parties/coalitions tie for the most valid national party list votes in this election, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the party/coalition whose listed name comes first in alphabetical order.

If any unlisted party wins the most national list votes in this election, or the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the official election results reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu). However, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may also suffice.

Note: If the Fidesz-KDNP does not contest this election with a joint national party list, Fidesz-KDNP margin of victory options will represent the national party list officially registered by Fidesz.

events.title: Hungary Election: Popular Vote Margin of Victory
events.description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026. Hungary uses a mixed electoral system in which most voters cast two votes: one for a candidate in a single-member constituency and one for a national party list. 

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two parties/coalitions in terms of national party list votes in this election.

For the purposes of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid national list votes received by the party/coalition whose national candidate list wins the most national list votes and the party/coalition whose national candidate list wins the second-most national list votes. Percentages of the valid votes received by each party/coalition will be determined by dividing the total number of valid national list votes each of the top two parties/coalitions receives by the sum of all valid national party list votes cast in the election.

This market will resolve solely based on national party list votes cast in this election. Votes cast for single-member constituencies will not be considered.

If two or more parties/coalitions tie for the most valid national party list votes in this election, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the party/coalition whose listed name comes first in alphabetical order.

If any unlisted party wins the most national list votes in this election, or the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the official election results reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu). However, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may also suffice.

Note: If the Fidesz-KDNP does not contest this election with a joint national party list, Fidesz-KDNP margin of victory options will represent the national party list officially registered by Fidesz.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls showing Tisza Party hovering at a slim 2-5% lead over Fidesz-KDNP in national surveys underpin the tight trader consensus, with no margin bin exceeding 20.5% probability for a Tisza blowout. Péter Magyar's momentum from the June EU parliamentary vote, where Tisza captured 30% amid corruption scandals eroding Fidesz support, keeps opposition viable despite Viktor Orbán's incumbency edge, media dominance, and rural strongholds. Economic pressures like inflation and frozen EU funds add volatility, while fragmented minor parties cap decisive separation. Upcoming candidate announcements and winter economic data could widen gaps, but historical base rates favor narrow victories in polarized Hungarian parliamentary races.

question: Will another outcome occur in the 2026 Hungarian Parliamentary election?
description:
Parliamentary elections are scheduled to be held in Hungary on April 12, 2026. Hungary uses a mixed electoral system in which most voters cast two votes: one for a candidate in a single-member constituency and one for a national party list. 

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two parties/coalitions in terms of national party list votes in this election.

For the purposes of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid national list votes received by the party/coalition whose national candidate list wins the most national list votes and the party/coalition whose national candidate list wins the second-most national list votes. Percentages of the valid votes received by each party/coalition will be determined by dividing the total number of valid national list votes each of the top two parties/coalitions receives by the sum of all valid national party list votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

This market will resolve solely based on national party list votes cast in this election. Votes cast for single-member constituencies will not be considered.

If two or more parties/coalitions tie for the most valid national party list votes in this election, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the party/coalition whose listed name comes first in alphabetical order.

If any unlisted party wins the most national list votes in this election, or the results of this election are not definitively known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the official election results reported by the Hungarian government, specifically the Hungarian Election Authority (Nemzeti Választási Iroda) (valasztas.hu). However, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may also suffice.

Note: If the Fidesz-KDNP does not contest this election with a joint national party list, Fidesz-KDNP margin of victory options will represent the national party list officially registered by Fidesz.

question: Spread: Chelsea FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for April 1 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Chelsea FC" if Chelsea FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Arsenal WFC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on uefa.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Chelsea FC vs. Arsenal WFC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the UEFA Women's Champions League game, scheduled for April 1 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Chelsea's commanding home record at Kingsmeadow and recent head-to-head dominance—winning four of the last five WSL meetings against Arsenal—anchor trader consensus favoring the Blues in this pivotal top-of-table Women's Super League clash. Upsets remain possible in these high-stakes derbies.

question: Will the Iranian regime fall by April 30?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Islamic Republic of Iran’s current ruling regime is overthrown, collapsed, or otherwise ceases to govern by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This requires a broad consensus of reporting indicating that core structures of the Islamic Republic (e.g. the office of the Supreme Leader, the Guardian Council, IRGC control under clerical authority) have been dissolved, incapacitated, or replaced by a fundamentally different governing system or otherwise lost de facto power over a majority of the population of Iran. This could occur via revolution, civil war, military coup, or voluntary abdication, but only qualifies if the Islamic Republic no longer exercises sovereign power.

Routine political events such as elections, reforms, or leadership succession do not qualify. Internal coups or power shifts that preserve the Islamic Republic’s core structures also do not qualify. Only a clear break in continuity—such as a new provisional government, revolutionary council, or constitution replacing the Islamic Republic will qualify.

Partial loss of territory or challenges from rebel or exile groups will not qualify unless the Islamic Republic no longer administers the majority of the Iranian population within Iran. 

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus strongly favors the Iranian regime's survival past April 30, pricing "No" at 88.5%, due to the absence of mass protests, military defections, or elite fractures signaling imminent collapse. Recent October 2024 missile exchanges with Israel heightened tensions but triggered no domestic upheaval, with Tehran's security forces maintaining firm control amid contained dissent. Economic sanctions and inflation persist, yet loyalty among the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and Basij militia has stifled opposition, echoing the fade of 2022 Mahsa Amini unrest without systemic threat. No major catalysts like elections or leadership transitions loom by the deadline, reinforcing historical regime resilience against internal challenges.

question: Spain snap election called in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a snap election is called in Spain by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The calling of a snap election requires the formal dissolution of at least one house of the Spanish Parliament or another formal scheduling, according to the rules of the jurisdiction, of an election for all members of at least one house of the Spanish Parliament prior to their scheduled election at the end of their parliamentary term.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Spain; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Spain snap election called in 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Spain's minority government under Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez maintains stability without signals of imminent collapse, anchoring trader consensus at 75.5% against a snap election in 2026, as the next scheduled general election remains set for 2027. Recent passage of the 2025 national budget, secured via pacts with Catalan parties like Junts and ERC despite opposition from the Popular Party (PP) and Vox, has bolstered legislative confidence. Polls show PSOE holding steady amid economic growth, while Sánchez has publicly rejected early elections, focusing on policy delivery; absent a no-confidence vote or coalition rupture, traders see limited catalysts for dissolution in the coming year.

question: Canucks vs. Avalanche
description:
In the upcoming NHL game, scheduled for April 1 at 8:30PM ET:
If the Canucks win, the market will resolve to "Canucks".
If the Avalanche win, the market will resolve to "Avalanche".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Vancouver Canucks face Colorado Avalanche in a pivotal NHL matchup, with trader consensus heavily favoring the Avalanche at home due to their dominant 8-2-1 record in recent games and superior power play efficiency. Canucks' goaltender Thatcher Demko's status remains uncertain after missing practice with a minor ailment, per official injury report, potentially forcing Casey DeSmith into net where he's 4-3-1 lifetime vs. Colorado. Avalanche get a boost from Nathan MacKinnon's return from suspension, enhancing their top-line scoring threat. Recent form shows Canucks winning three straight but struggling on the road (2-4-1), while Colorado boasts a rested defense post-bye week. Watch for special teams edge, as Avalanche kill 85% of penalties lately.

eventMetadata.context_description: St. Louis Blues enter their road clash with the Los Angeles Kings as trader favorites, bolstered by a five-game win streak and stout defense allowing just 2.2 goals per game lately, contrasting the Kings' three straight losses amid goaltender Darcy Kuemper's shaky .885 save percentage. Official injury reports confirm Blues forward Pavel Buchnevich is day-to-day with a lower-body issue, potentially thinning their top line, while Kings center Anze Kopitar returns from suspension, boosting faceoff prowess. Home-ice tilts slightly toward LA historically (6-4 last 10 meetings), but Blues' rest advantage after a lighter schedule sharpens momentum; watch power-play efficiency, where St. Louis converts 28% versus Kings' porous 75% penalty kill.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anaheim Ducks enter their Pacific Division clash with the San Jose Sharks as trader favorites, driven by home-ice advantage at Honda Center and superior recent form, winning four of their last six amid a young roster's momentum. Sharks limp in on a three-game skid, plagued by defensive injuries including top blueliner Henry Thrun out long-term and shaky goaltending from Vitek Vanecek, conceding 3.8 goals per game. Ducks' attack, led by Trevor Zegras's return, exploits San Jose's league-worst penalty kill at 71%. Rest edge for Anaheim after a bye week contrasts Sharks' road fatigue from back-to-backs, though both teams' low-scoring tendencies keep implied probabilities tight around 55-45.

eventMetadata.context_description: Anaheim Ducks enter their Pacific Division clash with the San Jose Sharks as trader favorites, driven by home-ice advantage at Honda Center and superior recent form, winning four of their last six amid a young roster's momentum. Rest edge for Anaheim after a bye week contrasts Sharks' road fatigue from back-to-backs, though both teams' low-scoring tendencies keep implied probabilities tight around 55-45.

question: Will Jean-Luc Moudenc be the next mayor of Toulouse following the 2026 Toulouse municipal election?
description:
The 2026 Toulouse municipal election is scheduled for March 15, 2026, with a potential runoff on March 22. The Mayor of Toulouse will then be elected by the Toulouse Municipal Council elected in this election.

This market will resolve according to the next individual elected to be the Mayor of Toulouse following this election.

Interim, temporary, or caretaker mayors will not count.

If no such mayor has been elected by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the government of Toulouse; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jean-Luc Moudenc's near-certain trader consensus at 99.7% for next Toulouse mayor stems from his entrenched incumbency since 2004, commanding 2020 reelection victory with 52% in the runoff, and dominant polling for the 2026 municipal vote amid fragmented opposition. Recent developments, including his March 2024 announcement seeking a fourth term and weak primary showings by rivals like François Piquemal (left) and Lambert Meilhac (far-right), reinforce this lead, with no credible challenger emerging. Markets reflect skin-in-the-game bets on continuity in Toulouse's stable center-right governance. Realistic challenges include a surprise national political shift boosting extremes, personal scandal, or unified leftist coalition, though current evidence shows minimal momentum for such disruptions ahead of candidate filings.

question: Will Ilia Topuria fight Charles Oliveira next?
description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Arman Tsarukyan as the clear lightweight frontrunner at 42.1% to face Charles Oliveira next, anchored by his unanimous decision win over Oliveira at UFC 300 and top-contender status ahead of his UFC 309 bout with Mateusz Gamrot on December 7. Benoît Saint Denis follows at 33.6%, fueled by his dominant first-round TKO of Drew Dober in August at UFC Abu Dhabi, extending his surge in the 155-pound ranks. Justin Gaethje's 15.2% reflects their rivalry—Oliveira dethroned him for interim gold—while Paddy Pimblett's 12.6% gains from his UFC 304 submission of Bobby Green and direct callout. Absent official UFC matchmaking, odds mirror recent lightweight momentum and division hierarchy.

question: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair nomination withdrawn by May 15?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Kevin Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve is withdrawn by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Formal withdrawal of Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve is required for a “Yes” resolution. Rejection of Warsh’s nomination by the United States Senate will not count.

If Kevin Warsh is formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve by the Senate, this market will immediately resolve to “No”.

If Warsh's nomination remains pending in the Senate through May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from Kevin Warsh, the Trump Administration, and the United States Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Kevin Warsh Fed Chair nomination withdrawn by May 15?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Kevin Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve is withdrawn by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Formal withdrawal of Warsh’s nomination as Chair of the Federal Reserve is required for a “Yes” resolution. Rejection of Warsh’s nomination by the United States Senate will not count.

If Kevin Warsh is formally confirmed as Chair of the Federal Reserve by the Senate, this market will immediately resolve to “No”.

If Warsh's nomination remains pending in the Senate through May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from Kevin Warsh, the Trump Administration, and the United States Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors no withdrawal of a Kevin Warsh Federal Reserve Chair nomination by May 15, reflecting the absence of any formal nomination to date. President-elect Donald Trump has interviewed Warsh, a former Fed governor known for inflation-hawkish views, amid discussions of replacing Jerome Powell whose term ends May 2026, but official announcements await Senate confirmation processes post-inauguration on January 20. Recent reports highlight competing candidates like Kevin Hassett, diluting Warsh's frontrunner status without triggering a nomination. High confidence stems from tight timelines and procedural hurdles; realistic shifts could arise from rapid post-election nomination followed by bipartisan opposition or market volatility prompting reversal, though traders see slim odds.

question: Will Israel reopen its embassy in Iran in 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Israeli government announces the reopening/opening of any embassy or consulate in Iran, or if such a reopening/opening is otherwise confirmed, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement made within this market’s timeframe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether an actual embassy or consulate opening subsequently takes place within the timeframe.

Any opening of an Israeli embassy or consulate in Iran will qualify regardless of its exact location.

Announcements that do not clearly commit to opening an embassy or consulate, such as general diplomatic statements, exploratory comments, or partial/conditional steps, will not count.

The resolution source will be official statements from the Government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: Will Israel reopen its embassy in Iran in 2026?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Israeli government announces the reopening/opening of any embassy or consulate in Iran, or if such a reopening/opening is otherwise confirmed, by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

An official announcement made within this market’s timeframe will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether an actual embassy or consulate opening subsequently takes place within the timeframe.

Any opening of an Israeli embassy or consulate in Iran will qualify regardless of its exact location.

Announcements that do not clearly commit to opening an embassy or consulate, such as general diplomatic statements, exploratory comments, or partial/conditional steps, will not count.

The resolution source will be official statements from the Government of Israel; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hostile Israel-Iran relations, severed since the 1979 Islamic Revolution when Israel closed its Tehran embassy, form the core driver of trader consensus pricing "No" at 88.5% for reopening in 2026. No official diplomatic channels exist amid Iran's support for proxies like Hezbollah and Hamas attacking Israel, plus direct 2024 missile exchanges—Tehran's April barrage and Israel's October strikes on Iranian military sites. Recent escalations, including Israel's expanded operations against Hezbollah, underscore zero momentum toward normalization, with Israeli leaders prioritizing Iran's nuclear threat over engagement. Absent any thaw signals, traders see negligible path to embassy restoration.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Max Holloway's next UFC bout hinges on the lack of an official announcement from UFC brass, fueling a dead heat between Dan Hooker at 51% and Benoît Saint Denis at 51%, as both lightweight contenders have issued direct callouts amid Holloway's BMF title reign and knockout of Justin Gaethje at UFC 300. Hooker's emphatic submission of Jalin Turner at UFC 305 in Perth amplified his trash talk toward the Hawaiian, tapping into rivalry hype, while Saint Denis, fresh off a gritty loss to Islam Makhachev, eyes a statement win to rebound his contender status. This tight race reflects the lightweight division's logjam— with Arman Tsarukyan and Mateusz Gamrot title-bound—leaving traders evenly split on Holloway's path to another high-profile lightweight clash before any featherweight return.

question: Will Billy Butcher die in "The Boys: Season 5"?
description:
"The Boys: Season 5" is scheduled to air weekly, beginning April 8, 2026, and ending with the finale on May 20, 2026

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the specified character dies during "The Boys: Season 5". Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A qualifying death must show the specified character dead on screen, or otherwise that character’s death must clearly be stated to have occurred, even if offscreen (e.g., characters confirm their death in conversation, the funeral of a character occurs, etc.). 

If a death is reversed through a revival, resurrection, or reanimation that occurs after the character has died, that death will still qualify. Deaths occurring in any timeline or dimension depicted in "The Boys: Season 5" will count toward resolution.

Flashback scenes showing a character alive before their death, as well as dream sequences, hallucinations, or visions of dead characters, will not affect resolution. Characters who were already confirmed dead before "The Boys: Season 5" begins will not count toward this market’s resolution.

If a character’s fate is deliberately left ambiguous, it will not qualify toward the resolution of this market. Only deaths confirmed by the end of "The Boys: Season 5" will qualify.

Only events depicted in official "The Boys: Season 5" episodes will count toward resolution. Post-credits scenes count if they are part of the official episode, but supplementary materials such as podcasts, comics, or other media do not count unless they are explicitly referenced within the show itself. The market will resolve after the final episode of "The Boys: Season 5" is released.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for major character deaths in The Boys Season 5 hinges on showrunner Eric Kripke's confirmation of it as the explosive final season, with production kicking off in November 2024 after Season 4's chaotic finale left Homelander ascendant, Butcher compromised by his V-induced tumor, and key supes like Firecracker and Sister Sage in precarious spots. Historical patterns—Season 3's shocking kills like Starlight's ally and Black Noir—fuel bets on high-profile exits, especially for arcs demanding closure like Hughie or A-Train. Odds reflect fan theories from comic parallels and actor interviews hinting at farewells, but secret scripting keeps outcomes unpredictable until 2026 release; watch for early set leaks shifting implied probabilities.

question: Will Jon Rothstein tweet "This is March" ≤ 40 times during March Madness?
description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Jon Rothstein (@JonRothstein) mentions "This is March" in a tweet posted on X between March 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and April 7, 2026, 2:00 AM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts, and reposts will count.

Only posts live at the listed time will count toward the final tally. Deleted posts or posts that were reposted and later un-reposted will NOT count.

Text posted in images, memes, video, or any attachments will not qualify towards the final count.

Any plural or possessive forms of the listed term, as well as variance in capitalizations, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will NOT count.

Extraneous symbols being inserted into a word (ex: Th1s, for "this") will disqualify it from counting toward the final count.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: marchhh or mar., for ‘March’), will not count towards the final count, regardless of context or intent.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. marchmadness mentions are not a compound word for "march").

Instances where both the quote and the quoted post contain the relevant phrase will qualify as one mention. (e.g., if John quotes a post which contains "This is March" with his own text containing "This is March", it will qualify as one mention.)

If the relevant data is unavailable by April 20, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be Jon Rothstein’s verified X account: @JonRothstein.

Please note, only the @JonRothstein verified X account counts for this market, regardless of the URL for this profile. If Jon Rothstein posts from another account, it has no bearing on the resolution of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Traders lean heavily toward Jon Rothstein exceeding 80 "This is March" tweets during the 2024 NCAA Tournament, with that outcome at 41% implied probability, buoyed by his already 52 such posts through the Elite Eight amid buzzer-beaters like NC State's upset run and Purdue's comeback. Historical patterns show Rothstein averaging over 100 in recent March Madness editions during high-drama stretches, and with Final Four matchups featuring Purdue vs. NC State and UConn vs. Alabama—both ripe for chaos—the wisdom of crowds anticipates sustained frenzy. Lower buckets like 51-60 (22.5%) gain from potential early Final Four lulls, but no major quieters have emerged to shift sentiment downward.

question: Will Anthropic make a deal with the Pentagon?
description:
In February 2026, the Pentagon announced it would designate Anthropic as a national security supply chain risk after Anthropic refused to remove AI safety restrictions from its acceptable use policy. Donald Trump subsequently directed all federal agencies to cease using Anthropic's technologies, with a six-month phase-out period for agencies such as the Department of Defense which are actively using Anthropic's products.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if Anthropic and the United States Department of Defense (DOD/Department of War) reach any commercial agreement to allow for the use of Claude or other Anthropic artificial intelligence models by DOD employees by April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A commercial agreement between Anthropic and a broader set of the US government that grants usage of Anthropic AI models to DOD employees will count. Agreements or designations which allow Anthropic to offer its services to the DOD, but do not constitute an effective agreement for Anthropic to do so, however, will not count (e.g the inclusion of Anthropic on a Master Service Agreement or Indefinite Delivery Indefinite Quantity contract would not count).

An official announcement of a qualifying agreement, made within this market’s timeframe, will count, regardless of whether or when the agreement actually goes into effect.

Official announcements that the previously agreed contract between Anthropic and the DOD will be fully or partially reinstated, or otherwise will continue without impediment, will count, so long as this includes extended use of Anthropic AI models by DOD employees beyond any designated phase-out period. 

Continued use of Anthropic technologies by DOD employees without a qualifying agreement (e.g. during a 6 month phase-out period) will not count. A court ruling that the designation of Anthropic as a supply chain risk is unlawful will not qualify for a “Yes” resolution unless it is accompanied by a reinstatement of Anthropic's DOD contract or a new qualifying Anthropic-DOD agreement.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from Anthropic and the United States federal government; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices an Anthropic-Pentagon deal at just 14.5% likelihood, reflecting the AI firm's unwavering safety-first ethos that explicitly limits military applications to non-offensive uses. CEO Dario Amodei has publicly drawn red lines against deals enabling lethal autonomous weapons, contrasting sharply with OpenAI's recent DoD collaborations on classified projects. eventMetadata.context_description: Olympiacos emerges as trader consensus favorite to reach the UEFA Europa Conference League final after stunning Aston Villa 4-2 in the first-leg semifinal at home, leveraging El Kaabi's brace and defensive resilience. Fiorentina sits level at 2-2 from their trip to Club Brugge, positioning them strongly for the return leg at the Stadio Artemio Franchi with last season's finalists' experience. Second legs on May 2 highlight Villa's Villa Park comeback potential against a two-goal deficit amid Unai Emery's squad depth concerns, while Brugge must overcome Mandragora's late equalizer. Momentum favors the Greek and Italian sides per implied probabilities, tempered by Conference League's history of aggregate shocks.

question: Will BIG qualify to IEM Cologne Major 2026?
description:
The IEM Cologne Major 2026 is scheduled to take place from June 2 to June 21, 2026.

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for IEM Cologne Major 2026.If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to IEM Cologne Major 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official ESL / Valve rules.

If IEM Cologne Major 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of IEM Cologne Major 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ESL website (https://pro.eslgaming.com/tour/cs/cologne/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Cologne) may also be used.

question: Markwayne Mullin confirmed as DHS Secretary by March 31, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Markwayne Mullin is confirmed as the United States Secretary of Homeland Security by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If Markwayne Mullin is formally nominated for the role, and his nomination is then formally withdrawn, or if the Senate rejects the nomination, this market will immediately resolve to “No”.

The Senate must confirm Markwayne Mullin as Secretary of Homeland Security for this market to resolve to "Yes". Recess appointments or assumption of the position in an acting capacity without Senate confirmation will not count toward a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Markwayne Mullin confirmed as DHS Secretary by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Markwayne Mullin is confirmed as the United States Secretary of Homeland Security by the specified date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If Markwayne Mullin is formally nominated for the role, and his nomination is then formally withdrawn, or if the Senate rejects the nomination, this market will immediately resolve to “No”.

The Senate must confirm Markwayne Mullin as Secretary of Homeland Security for this market to resolve to "Yes". Recess appointments or assumption of the position in an acting capacity without Senate confirmation will not count toward a "Yes" resolution.

The primary resolution source for this market is official information from the U.S. Senate; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probability for Sen. Markwayne Mullin's confirmation as DHS Secretary, primarily driven by President-elect Trump's nomination of South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem for the role on November 12, with no indication of Mullin as an alternate or replacement. Mullin, an Oklahoma Republican senator and Trump ally, has voiced support for Trump's cabinet picks but holds no official nomination. Recent Senate Republican gains bolster approval odds for nominees generally, yet Noem's hearing—expected in January—remains the key catalyst, potentially resolving DHS leadership before inauguration. Without a shift in Trump's selections, Mullin's path appears closed, underscoring nomination as the decisive factor in confirmation markets.

question: Will no one leave the Trump Cabinet before 2027?
description:
This market will resolve based on the next individual announced to leave the Trump Cabinet, or who otherwise ceases to be a member of the administration. If no one leaves by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “None before 2027”.

An announcement of an individual's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the resignation/removal takes effect.

If multiple individuals announce departures or are removed at the same time, the market will resolve to the individual who actually leaves office first. If they leave simultaneously, the market will resolve to the individual whose last name comes first alphabetically.

For the purposes of this market, the Cabinet includes Vice President, the heads of the 15 executive departments, as well as the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), the President’s Chief of Staff, the Director of National Intelligence (DNI), the Director of the Office of Management and Budget (OMB), the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency (CIA), the United States Trade Representative (USTR), the Ambassador to the United Nations, the Chair of the Council of Economic Advisers (CEA), and the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA). Acting officials serving in these roles are not included.
